_schema_note: Ground truth findings for the session 5 doc fixture. Extracted manually from the ## Post-Approval Hallucination Audit section of akagilnc-main-design-20260411-174843.md (H1-H10). Used by eval/run_eval.sh to compute recall of a reviewer pipeline against eval/session5_fixture.md. Matching is keyword-substring on reviewer finding text (case-insensitive). If ANY keyword in keywords_any appears in a reviewer finding, that finding is attributed to this H#. A single reviewer finding can match multiple H#s.

findings.location_hint: B6, Hour 44-48; mentions of comic-compositor.py and --panels=1
findings.description: Doc claims comic-compositor.py takes a --panels=1 CLI flag. Reality: the script has no CLI flags, it reads a JSON object from stdin with a 'panels' array, and compute_grid(n) already handles n=1. No modifications needed.

Doc says refactor src/workflow/run-tick.ts to accept personaId. Reality: run-tick.ts is already a pure dependency-injection function (RunTickDeps). The XHS-specific wiring lives in src/cli.ts inside async function runSingleTick. The refactor should target cli.ts, not run-tick.ts.

findings.location_hint: Hour 22-28; mentions of post-decider thin passthrough
findings.description: Doc claims decide.ts can be a thin passthrough to post-decider with persona config. Reality: post-decider.ts has a Zod enum imageMode hardcoded to exactly ['screenshot','text_card'], and the prompt is hardcoded Chinese referencing 小红书 directly. Needs fork or parameterization, not thin passthrough. Adds 3-4 hours.

findings.location_hint: Constraints, B6; mentions of piece-critique reuse
findings.description: Doc claims piece-critique engine is reusable (true for the engine layer) but also claims the rules layer is persona-neutral (false). All 7 HEURISTIC_FNS are CJK-specific: character-count thresholds, Chinese regex patterns, CJK concrete-noun lists, [\u4e00-\u9fa5] title-body mismatch regex. Every heuristic needs English rewrite. Adds 6-8 hours.

Doc claims editor-pass is reusable without rewrite. Reality: DROP_MARKERS and PREFIX_REWRITES are module-level const Chinese strings with no configuration parameter. applyEditorialPass(body) takes no config. Needs module-level parameterization or fork. Adds 4-6 hours.

findings.location_hint: Problem Statement L16 vs L99 and L421
findings.description: Line 16 says 'daily-cron GitHub Actions'; lines 99 and 421 correctly say 'hourly check'. postflow's actual cron is '0 * * * *' (hourly) with content-gate filtering. L16 is wrong.

Doc calls betaQuantile(p, alpha, beta). The npm package @stdlib/stats-base-dists-beta-quantile actually exports a function named quantile, not betaQuantile. Argument order is correct, only the name is wrong.

findings.location_hint: L3 Algorithm L331 + Success Criteria L396
findings.description: Every Beta quantile number claimed in the doc is wrong. Actual scipy.stats.beta.ppf(0.975, 1, n+1) values: Beta(1,19)=0.1765 (not 0.158), Beta(1,21)=0.1611 (not 0.143). First pivot under threshold 0.15 fires at Beta(1,23)=0.1482 which is 22 silences, not Beta(1,21)=20 silences. Success Criteria test data using 'inject Beta(1,21)' would NOT trigger the pivot and the L3 test suite would be red on first build. CRITICAL severity because it would block implementation.

findings.location_hint: Open Questions Q1-Q10 vs Dependencies/Assignment section Q5-Q7
findings.description: Two independent Q numbering systems in the document collide. Open Questions section uses Q1-Q10 in semantic order; Dependencies + Assignment sections reuse 'Q5/Q6/Q7' as task-order labels. Open Questions Q7 is 'B3 Reddit signal verification' while Dependencies Q7 is 'subreddit allowlist final selection' — different items with identical labels.

File name x-editor-pass.ts uses the session 4 X-prefix convention from when X (Twitter) was the target platform. Session 5 dropped X entirely. The file should be named editor-pass-en.ts or live under src/personas/robot-en/.
